title: pasta: HTTP downloads get truncated (UrBackup web UI). slirp4netns works fine.
body:
### Issue Description

HTTP responses get cut short from the urbackup http server container only when using pasta. Some of the urbackup web interface CSS/JS files get cut short (still have a valid Content-Length header). Chrome shows net::ERR_CONTENT_LENGTH_MISMATCH.

If I switch the exact same container to Network=slirp4netns, the problem disappears. Also, if I serve similarly sized files with nginx over pasta, I cannot reproduce the issue.

No reverse proxy involved. Browser and second host used to test are on the same LAN.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run urbackup with pasta: `podman run --rm --name urbackup2   --network pasta:--map-gw   -p 8880:55414   docker.io/uroni/urbackup-server:latest
`
2. From another host, fetch a UI asset (might need to try a few times to hit the bug): `curl --http1.1 -v http://192.168.10.102:8880/css/bootstrap.chash-2f624089c65f12185e79925bc5a7fc42.min.css   -o /tmp/ur-pasta-keep.css   -w '\nsize_download=%`{size_download}\n\n'


### Describe the results you received

Sometimes content is received OK, sometimes truncated.

Examples (4x requests with bytes missing, 2x OK)
```
[root@manage ~]# curl --http1.1 -v http://192.168.10.102:8880/css/bootstrap.chash-2f624089c65f12185e79925bc5a7fc42.min.css   -o /tmp/ur-pasta-keep.css   -w '\nsize_download=%{size_download}\n\n'

kvm

### Additional information

```
[urbackup@c2 ~]$ podman run --rm --name urbackup2   --network slirp4netns   -p 8880:55414   docker.io/uroni/urbackup-server:latest
```

    581 size_download=121260

582
[root@manage ~]#
```

comments.body:
>     package: passt-0^20250217.ga1e48a0-5.el10_0.x86_64

Upstream only supports the latest versions so please test the lastest version of pasta as it might have been fixed already.

cc @sbrivio-rh

Same issue also on pasta 2025_09_19.623dbf6-4-gf4729be

Description  : passt implements a translation layer between a Layer-2 network interface and

             : replacement for Slirp.

             : for network namespaces: traffic is forwarded using a tap interface inside the
             : namespace, without the need to create further interfaces on the host, hence not
             : requiring any capabilities or privileges.

I believe I'm hitting this as well on an internal web-based service running on a RHEL 9 host.

podman-5.4.0-12.el9_6.x86_64
passt-0^20250217.ga1e48a0-10.el9_6.x86_64

Though our reproducer is quite a bit more complex, the symptoms appear the same.  Our Apache logs show XYZ bytes being sent for a client request.  Then on the client side, a python tool is throwing an `incompleteRead` exception.  Attempting the same client-side request using curl, shows fewer than XYZ bytes being delivered before connection close.

Similarly, switching back to `slirp4netns` seems to resolve the issue.

> I believe I'm hitting this as well on an internal web-based service running on a RHEL 9 host.
> 
> podman-5.4.0-12.el9_6.x86_64 passt-0^20250217.ga1e48a0-10.el9_6.x86_64

On those versions, the issue might actually be different.

> Though our reproducer is quite a bit more complex, the symptoms appear the same. Our Apache logs show XYZ bytes being sent for a client request. Then on the client side, a python tool is throwing an `incompleteRead` exception. Attempting the same client-side request using curl, shows fewer than XYZ bytes being delivered before connection close.

Thanks for the report. Actually, I just managed to reproduce this with the original UrBackup container reproducer, so I'm finally debugging this further. I "just" had to pick two hosts with approximately 10ms round-trip time between them (1ms as I tried first won't quite do it).

> I "just" had to pick two hosts with approximately 10ms round-trip time between them (1ms as I tried first won't quite do it).

Fascinating!  In our case as well, the problem seemed to mostly be affecting remote clients accessing our app over a VPN.

> I just sent a [patch](https://archives.passt.top/passt-dev/20250930205132.885225-1-sbrivio@redhat.com/) for this, and the commit message explains the problem in detail.

It looks like I completely misinterpreted the issue: I thought we'd be sending a FIN segment with an acknowledgement sequence less than the sequence we actually received and queued to the socket (here, to the client).

It turns out, instead, that we actually have a short write to the socket (sendmsg() returning less than what we wrote), as the socket buffer is full, so we didn't accept those missing bytes, and it's correct that we don't acknowledge them, of course.

However, as we don't acknowledge them, we should ignore the FIN segment and not switch to the CLOSE-WAIT state right away: we need the server to send us that data again, and then send it on.

I just posted a [series](https://archives.passt.top/passt-dev/20251002000646.2136202-1-sbrivio@redhat.com/) fixing that, and a few minor assorted issues I found while investigating.

@hakong, would you be so kind as to re-test with that series? `b4 shazam` applies series as well, just drop the previous patch:

    git reset HEAD~1

and apply the new series instead:

    b4 shazam https://archives.passt.top/passt-dev/20251002000646.2136202-1-sbrivio@redhat.com/

then build, install, etc. Thanks.

Applying: tcp: Don't consider FIN flags with mismatching sequence
Applying: tcp: On partial send (incomplete sendmsg()), request a retransmission right away

[root@c2 passt]#
